description_long: This sculpture depicts a classic Egyptian sphinx, featuring the body of a reclining lion and the head of a human adorned with a nemes headdress and a uraeus (cobra) on the forehead. The figure is rendered in a smooth, stone-like material with subtle gradations of gray and white, giving it a weathered yet dignified appearance. The musculature of the lion's body is well-defined, with strong forelegs extended forward and the hindquarters tucked beneath. The face is serene and symmetrical, with stylized features typical of ancient Egyptian art. The headdress is ribbed and flows down the sides of the head, while the tail curls around the right haunch. The overall form is solid and monumental, suggesting a sense of permanence and guardianship.
description: A detailed Egyptian sphinx sculpture with a human head and lion's body, rendered in smooth gray stone with a ribbed headdress and coiled tail.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the human face, headdress, and extended forepaws of the sphinx.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the head, ribbed headdress, muscular body, and curled tail.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the symmetry of the headdress, back, and positioning of the limbs.
description_view_3: Right side view displaying the profile of the head, body, and tail, mirroring the left side.
attribute_subject_matter: Egyptian sphinx with human head and lion body
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
